title: Python 3.14, pygame-ce 2.5.7, SDL, Initialization, and the Main Loop · Language Clinic: Init Results
summary: A script imports an incompatible pygame distribution, opens a window during import, ignores failed modules and quit events, presents twice, and never cleans up. Predict player-visible behavior, state, units, timing, input, rendering, feedback, failure, accessibility, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the init results increment in owned runtime and minimal event-update-render loop. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-cas…
objectives: Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.
Explain how pygame calls into SDL and identify display, event, input, audio, timer, filesystem, and platform boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-asteroids-python cumulative accessible real-time game portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python simulation models and fixed fixtures only.
Native pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers,
GPU behavior, frame pacing, accessibility playtests, packaging, and production effects require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class SimulationPolicy:
    fixed_step_seconds: float = 1 / 120
    max_frame_seconds: float = 0.1
    max_updates_per_frame: int = 8
    logical_width: int = 960
    logical_height: int = 540


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class RunEvidence:
    revision: str
    seed: int
    input_trace_id: str
    runtime_id: str
    accessibility_profile: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python Asteroids portfolio ready"

Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-worked-asteroids-runtime-sdl-loop-3
# Competency: asteroids-worked-asteroids-runtime-sdl-loop-3.
# Case 5798: a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: cross both screen seams in one update and retain collision and rendering continuity.
def worked_asteroids_runtime_sdl_loop_3(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked3 = "worked3-3dekrow-5798"
    assert evidence_variant_worked3
    evidence_axes_worked3 = (
        "playerworked3",
        "seed5798",
        "input3dekrow",
        "frameworked35798",
        "state5798worked3",
        "playtest3dekrow5798",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked3)) == 6
    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"

Browser code must not claim native pygame-ce, SDL, window, controller, mixer, filesystem, package, GPU, installer, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, released pygame-ce 2.5.7, fixed and headless SDL fixtures, real devices, licensed assets, accessibility playtests, profiling, clean installations, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: asteroids-init-results-a1x9wv
# Competency: asteroids-init-results.
# Case 8469: an offline cross-platform release rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: retain player goal, revision, Python, pygame-ce, SDL, fixture, seed, input trace, viewport, and result identity.
# Changed case: replay the same input trace at 30, 60, and 144 presentation frames per second and compare simulation state.
def evidence_asteroids_init_results_a1x9wv(python_minor: int = 14, pygame_ce_minor: int = 7, initialized_modules: int = 5, quit_handled: bool = True, cleanup_called: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_a1x9wv = "a1x9wv-vw9x1a-8469"
    assert evidence_variant_a1x9wv
    evidence_axes_a1x9wv = (
        "playera1x9wv",
        "seed8469",
        "inputvw9x1a",
        "framea1x9wv8469",
        "state8469a1x9wv",
        "playtestvw9x1a8469",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_a1x9wv)) == 6
    if python_minor < 14 or pygame_ce_minor < 7:
        return False, python_minor, "unsupported-runtime"
    if initialized_modules < 3:
        return False, initialized_modules, "partial-init"
    if not quit_handled:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-quit"
    if not cleanup_called:
        return False, initialized_modules, "missing-cleanup"
    assert initialized_modules >= 3
    return True, initialized_modules, "owned-runtime"

steps.title: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Init Results model · Theory Init Results
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Init Results: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory Asteroids team handles case 3278 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.

Evidence target: Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Init Results model · Theory Init Results.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Init Results model · Theory Init Results, keep Init Results connected to executable Python game-state, timing, input, collision, accessibility, test, package, and release decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.

hints: For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Init Results model · Theory Init Results, name the player, revision, runtime, seed, input, time, coordinate, state, entity, collision, render, cue, accessibility, package, release, and desktop-transfer boundary, Init Results precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Init Results model · Theory Init Results, trace this concrete input and predict event, intent, simulation, collision, cleanup, rendering, cue, accessibility, failure, pause, rollback, recovery, and desktop evidence before reviewing it: A theory Asteroids team handles case 3278 during a low-power integrated-GPU frame-budget trial. Build deterministic Python evidence to own initialization, the event-render loop, quit handling, and cleanup under a versioned Python and SDL boundary; record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, playtest observation, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner; then run the packaged build with dummy video and audio drivers, missing optional sound, and a corrupted save. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real pygame-ce, SDL, windows, controllers, audio devices, assets, files, installers, frame timing, GPUs, packaging, and production behavior require explicit controlled desktop transfer gates. Competency probe: Initialize only required modules, inspect failures, create the display before dependent conversions, and preserve causal diagnostics.

For Asteroids, pygame-ce, and Python Defend the Init Results model · Theory Init Results, challenge the documented misconception with changed-frame-rate, stall, seam, input, device, collision, cue, accessibility, save, package, failure, recovery, and controlled desktop-transfer evidence: pygame.init returning is proof that every subsystem works.

checks.description: The cumulative python portfolio contains this competency’s named evidence increment.
checks.failureMessage: Add the exact evidence marker # Evidence: asteroids-init-results-a1x9wv.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: asteroids-init-results-a1x9wv(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_asteroids_init_results_a1x9wv\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?python_minor < 14)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?initialized_modules < 3)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not quit_handled)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?not cleanup_called)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
